message:
feat(connectors): cover multiple opencode + pi_agent installation layouts (issue #174)

Extend the OpenCode and pi-agent connectors so scanning picks up
every known installation layout, not just the single canonical
path each connector originally assumed.  Both changes are
motivated by issue #174, which reported that users with
non-default installs were getting empty scans even though their
sessions were plainly present on disk.

src/connectors/opencode.rs

Problem: OpenCode uses XDG-style paths on all platforms (including
macOS), so `~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db` is the canonical
location.  The previous code called `dirs::data_local_dir()`
first, which on macOS maps to `~/Library/Application Support` —
a location OpenCode never writes to.  That branch always missed
the real database on macOS and fell through to the other
candidates by chance.

Fix:
- sqlite_db_path() is now a thin wrapper over the new
  sqlite_db_candidates() helper: it walks candidates in priority
  order and returns the first one that exists, or None.
- New sqlite_db_candidates() -> Vec<PathBuf> returns every known
  location in priority order:
    1. $OPENCODE_SQLITE_DB env override (for tests + custom
       installs)
    2. $HOME/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db (XDG, canonical)
    3. $HOME/.config/opencode/opencode.db (XDG config fallback,
       for users who've moved the DB)
    4. dirs::data_local_dir()/opencode/opencode.db (platform
       default — intentionally *after* the XDG paths so macOS
       scans find the real file first)
    5. dirs::config_dir()/opencode/opencode.db (platform config)
  Deduplicates preserving order because multiple dirs helpers
  may resolve to the same path on a given platform.
- scan() now builds a `db_candidates` Vec that composes the
  caller's ctx.data_dir (if it points at a .db file or contains
  an opencode.db) with the full default candidate list.  Every
  candidate that exists is scanned, and a `scanned_dbs: HashSet`
  ensures the same canonicalized file isn't processed twice.
  This fixes the "scan root passed in by the user hides the
  canonical location" regression from #174.
- New `use std::collections::HashSet;` import for the dedup.

src/connectors/pi_agent.rs

Problem: pi_agent is distributed under two names with two
different storage locations:
  - pi-mono (badlogic/pi-mono) stores at ~/.pi/agent/
  - Oh My Pi (omp CLI) stores at ~/.omp/agent/
The connector only knew about the .pi/agent path, so users with
Oh My Pi installed got empty scans.

Fix:
- home() keeps its existing single-path semantics (used by legacy
  paths that need the primary home) and gains a doc pointer to
  the new helper.
- New default_homes() -> Vec<PathBuf>:
    - If $PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR is set, return [that] — explicit
      overrides remain a single-home answer so no hidden scan
      fan-out.
    - Otherwise return [~/.pi/agent, ~/.omp/agent] in priority
      order.
- is_pi_agent_dir matcher gains a .omp/agent check alongside
  the existing .pi/agent and pi-agent string matches.
- looks_like_root matcher gains a .omp/agent check in its
  substring list.
- scan() now iterates a `homes: Vec<PathBuf>` built from:
    - ctx.data_dir directly if is_pi_agent_dir
    - otherwise, default_homes() when default detection is
      enabled
  and processes each home independently, collecting their
  sessions into the same output.
- New unit test exercises a tmp directory containing both
  .pi/agent and .omp/agent fixture files and asserts both are
  discovered in a single scan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
